name: aria-session-manager
description: Session management via aria-api REST layer. All data in PostgreSQL (aria_engine.chat_sessions). List, prune stale, delete, stats.
author: Aria Team

tools.name: list_sessions
tools.description: List all sessions from the database. Returns id, agent_id, session_type, status, title, model, message_count, updated_at.

agent.description: Filter by agent name (main, analyst, aria-talk, aria-memeothy). Empty = all agents.

tools.name: delete_session
tools.description: Delete a session and all its messages from the database.

tools.name: prune_sessions
tools.description: Bulk-archive stale sessions older than a threshold in one atomic DB transaction (copy to archive tables → delete from working tables). Sessions remain accessible via list_archived_sessions. Use dry_run=true to preview counts without making changes.

max_age_minutes.description: Archive sessions last updated more than this many minutes ago (default: 60). Converted to hours internally — use multiples of 60 for exact thresholds.

dry_run.description: If true, return candidate count without archiving anything.

tools.name: get_session_stats
tools.description: Get summary statistics: total sessions, stale count, active count, breakdown by agent.

tools.name: cleanup_after_delegation
tools.description: Clean up a session after a sub-agent delegation completes. Calls delete_session internally.

session_id.description: The session ID of the completed delegation to clean up

tools.name: list_archived_sessions
tools.description: Browse archived sessions. These were pruned from chat but preserved for historical context. Aria can see past conversations here.

tools.name: cleanup_orphans
tools.description: Clean up ghost sessions (0 messages, stale >15min) from the database. Run this periodically.

dry_run.description: If true, report what would be cleaned without doing it
